original_hypothesis.title: Any registered user can grief their own staked NFT into an unrecoverable state if farming math reverts, because unstake is force-coupled to farm
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: unstakeMunchable is guarded by forceFarmPlots(msg.sender), which executes _farmPlots before allowing unstake. If any single staked token causes _farmPlots to revert (due to config mistakes, bonus underflow, tax underflow, or dependency revert), the user cannot unstake any of their tokens, locking all custodied NFTs for that account. Attackers can deliberately create such a condition for themselves (or get victims into it via landlord-side settings if those are unconstrained).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Coupling exit to a complex reward calculation increases lock risk. Even if the triggering condition is rare, it creates high-severity operational incidents when it hits (mass NFT lock for affected users).
deployment_assumptions: There exists at least one reachable revert condition in _farmPlots for a staker (e.g., PRICE_PER_PLOT==0, latestTaxRate>1e18, negative bonus wrap leading to downstream revert in AccountManager, or external dependency revert)
No alternative emergency withdrawal exists elsewhere
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LandManager.sol: unstakeMunchable uses modifier forceFarmPlots(msg.sender); _farmPlots has multiple external calls and arithmetic that can revert; no try/catch or bypass for exit.

proof_of_signal.pattern: modifier forceFarmPlots(_account){ _farmPlots(_account); _; } combined with unstakeMunchable(... ) forceFarmPlots(msg.sender); _farmPlots contains unchecked arithmetic and external calls that can revert

preconditions: User has at least one staked NFT (custodied in LandManager)
_farmPlots reverts for that user's staked set (any one token reverting blocks the whole call)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Users report they cannot unstake; transaction traces show revert inside _farmPlots
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Often triggered by misconfig/dependency issues; may be triggered by an attacker-landlord if tax is unconstrained
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: As long as revert condition persists

operational_questions: What are all current revert causes inside _farmPlots on production config?
Can a user selectively unstake a token without farming all tokens first?
Are there guardrails in AccountManager.updatePlayer to prevent huge deltas or negative-derived values?
validation_requirements: Fuzz _farmPlots across config space and realistic attribute ranges
Simulate user with multiple staked tokens where one becomes 'poison' and blocks exit
Check for any admin rescue function in other managers

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Create a condition that makes farming revert (e.g., select a vulnerable attribute combo if bonuses misconfigured; or rely on PRICE_PER_PLOT=0 scenario)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Attempt unstake; observe permanent lock due to forced farming
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Operational impact manifests as locked NFTs and potential reputational damage

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Locked user NFTs; support escalations; potential emergency upgrade
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High without an emergency withdrawal path
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Unstaking and any actions using forceFarmPlots

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Reverts from unstakeMunchable with traces into _farmPlots; spikes after config changes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Accounts with NFTs held by LandManager but no successful exit over long periods
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Poison-token patterns (a specific realm/rarity repeatedly involved)

validation_result.impact_summary: The unstakeMunchable exit path is force-coupled to _farmPlots via the forceFarmPlots modifier. At least one concrete revert condition exists in _farmPlots (timestamp underflow when a landlord's plot count decreases), permanently locking user NFTs with no emergency withdrawal.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis identifies a real structural code defect: unstakeMunchable uses the forceFarmPlots(msg.sender) modifier which calls _farmPlots before any unstaking logic executes. _farmPlots iterates over ALL staked tokens for the caller and performs complex arithmetic and external calls for each. If any single computation reverts, the entire unstake transaction reverts.

I identified a concrete, reachable revert path: When a landlord's locked value decreases (reducing their plot count), _farmPlots detects the invalid plot and sets timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated. If this lastUpdated timestamp is before the staker's lastToilDate (which is the common case since stakers typically stake AFTER the landlord's metadata was last updated), then `timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate` underflows in Solidity 0.8.25's checked arithmetic, causing a revert.

Additional revert vectors include: (1) PRICE_PER_PLOT == 0 causing division by zero in _getNumPlots, (2) REALM_BONUSES/RARITY_BONUSES array out-of-bounds access, (3) overflow from negative bonus cast producing massive uint256 values, (4) external dependency reverts from accountManager, lockManager, or nftAttributesManager.

No emergency withdrawal function exists in the contract. While the contract is upgradeable, that is not a code-level safeguard.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The forceFarmPlots modifier at lines ~38-41 directly couples _farmPlots to unstakeMunchable. The timestamp underflow in _farmPlots occurs when the dirty condition triggers (lines ~241-244) and the subsequent subtraction (line ~260) underflows. The contract has no emergency withdrawal function - exhaustive review of all external/public functions confirms only unstakeMunchable can return NFTs, and it requires forceFarmPlots to succeed.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This modifier forces _farmPlots execution before any function that uses it (stakeMunchable, unstakeMunchable, transferToUnoccupiedPlot). If _farmPlots reverts, the guarded function cannot execute.
The only function that returns NFTs to users. It uses forceFarmPlots modifier, so if _farmPlots reverts, NFTs are permanently locked.

When a landlord's plot count decreases, timestamp is set to plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated. If lastUpdated < lastToilDate (common when user staked after the landlord's metadata was last set), the subtraction underflows in Solidity 0.8.25 checked arithmetic, causing a revert that propagates to block unstaking.

calling_sequence: _farmPlots iterates over all staked tokens via munchablesStaked[mainAccount]
For a token where landlord's plot count has decreased: _getNumPlots(landlord) < _toiler.plotId evaluates true
timestamp is set to plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated (which predates the stake)
Solidity 0.8.25 checked arithmetic panics with underflow
Entire transaction reverts; unstake never executes; NFT remains locked
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: unstakeMunchable → forceFarmPlots modifier → _farmPlots → dirty flag branch sets timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated → (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) underflows when lastUpdated < lastToilDate → revert propagates to block unstake. This is reachable when: (1) user staked at time T2, (2) landlord's plotMetadata.lastUpdated == T1 where T1 < T2, (3) landlord's locked value decreases making the plot invalid. The landlord does not need to call updatePlotMetadata after reducing value — the plot count is dynamically computed from lockManager.getLockedWeightedValue.

deployment_assumptions_checked: There exists at least one reachable revert condition in _farmPlots for a staker (e.g., PRICE_PER_PLOT==0, latestTaxRate>1e18, negative bonus wrap leading to downstream revert in AccountManager, or external dependency revert)
No alternative emergency withdrawal exists elsewhere
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts (upgrade-proxy-contract.ts) show LandManager is deployed behind a proxy and upgraded. No emergency withdrawal function is deployed or referenced. The _reconfigure function uses wrong StorageKeys for numeric config values (StorageKey.LockManager for MIN_TAX_RATE, StorageKey.NFTOverlord for PRICE_PER_PLOT), which could cause PRICE_PER_PLOT to be 0 and trigger division by zero. Scripts don't validate config values.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Concrete scenario: (1) Landlord has plotMetadata.lastUpdated = T1 from a previous updatePlotMetadata call. (2) User stakes NFT at time T2 > T1, setting lastToilDate = T2, on plotId P. (3) Landlord withdraws some locked value from lockManager, reducing available plots below P+1. No metadata update occurs since plot count is dynamically computed. (4) User attempts to unstake. forceFarmPlots runs _farmPlots. For the staked token: _getNumPlots(landlord) < plotId is true, so timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated = T1. Then (T1 - T2) underflows since T1 < T2. Transaction reverts. (5) User's NFT is permanently locked. This requires no attacker — it can happen naturally. A malicious landlord could deliberately trigger this by first setting up plots, waiting for stakers, then withdrawing locked value.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. User NFTs (ERC-721 assets with real value) can become permanently locked in the LandManager contract. The condition can arise naturally (landlord reduces locked value) or be deliberately triggered by a malicious landlord. All operations that use forceFarmPlots (stake, unstake, transfer) are blocked. The only recovery path would be a protocol upgrade, which requires admin action and may take significant time. Multiple users could be affected simultaneously if a popular landlord's locked value decreases.
recommended_mitigations: Add an emergency unstake function that bypasses farming: function emergencyUnstake(uint256 tokenId) external notPaused { ... } that skips _farmPlots and forfeits pending rewards
Wrap the farming logic in a try/catch or handle errors gracefully per-token so one reverting token doesn't block the entire iteration
Add a check in _farmPlots that if timestamp < _toiler.lastToilDate, set schnibblesTotal = 0 instead of reverting
Fix the dirty flag check to use <= instead of < to catch the boundary case correctly
Consider making _farmPlots skip tokens that would revert rather than reverting the entire call
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LandManager with mock dependencies. 2. Configure a landlord with locked value giving N plots. 3. Set plotMetadata.lastUpdated to T1 via updatePlotMetadata. 4. At T2 > T1, stake an NFT on plotId = N-1. 5. Reduce landlord's locked value in mockLockManager so _getNumPlots returns N-1 (invalidating plotId N-1). 6. Call unstakeMunchable. 7. Observe revert due to underflow in (T1 - T2). 8. Verify no other function can retrieve the NFT.
uncertainties: The exact behavior of configStorage.getUint with address-type StorageKeys is unknown (could return 0 or the address cast to uint256), which affects whether PRICE_PER_PLOT==0 is a real production concern
The lockManager implementation is not in scope — unclear exactly how/when getLockedWeightedValue changes
Whether the protocol has admin-level emergency mechanisms outside LandManager (e.g., direct proxy storage manipulation) that could recover locked NFTs
additional_testing_needed: Fuzz test _farmPlots with varying config values (PRICE_PER_PLOT, REALM_BONUSES, RARITY_BONUSES) to identify all revert paths
Integration test: simulate landlord reducing locked value after stakers are positioned, verify unstake reverts
Test with actual configStorage to determine what values the wrong StorageKeys return
Verify whether accountManager.updatePlayer can itself revert under edge conditions (e.g., massive schnibbles values)
